items.question: What is JSX and how does the browser run it?
items.answer: JSX is a syntax extension that lets you write HTML-like markup in JavaScript. Browsers don't understand it; a compiler (Babel/SWC/esbuild) transforms it into React.createElement calls (or the automatic jsx runtime) that produce React elements.

items.question: Why does React need a key prop on lists?
items.answer: Keys give each list item a stable identity so React can match elements between renders and update/move/remove only what changed, rather than re-creating the whole list. Use stable unique ids, not array indexes when the list can reorder.
